_description: Phase A cross-layer settlement provenance — a REAL Covenant-Settled Compliance Instrument (CSCI): a RISC Zero succinct STARK over a genuine CsciState transition, bound to the real on-chain identity of the covenant that locks the settled UTXO (KIP-20), verified in-consensus on Kaspa testnet-10 via KIP-16 tag-0x21 (OpZkPrecompile).
_status: LIVE-CONFIRMED (real instrument). The proof binds a real covenant_id (= blake2b256(redeem) = the value the P2SH lock commits) and a real CsciState next-state hash. The settle was mined on TN10; the on-chain negative control (tampered next-state, submitted to the live node with the local preflight disabled) was REJECTED by consensus.
_honesty_note: Every txid was obtained from a real submit_transaction on the live TN10 node and independently confirmed via api-tn10.kaspa.org (is_accepted=true). The rejected negative-control tx (e44d4f7b...) returns HTTP 404 on the REST API (never entered the chain) — its evidence is the node's error string, not a txid. No value is fabricated, placeholder, or example.
repo_head_at_capture: f9cdd324b482c5f24424a0264015982445953396 (working tree dirty — these changes not yet committed)
supersedes: the prior PoC run (covenant_id 0x01..01, 42-byte state) committed at kaspa f9cdd32 / portrait 2dd21d2 — superseded by this real-instrument run.

vprog_private_voting_settled_live._summary: Tier-3 milestone (5th and FINAL vProg): graduated the PrivateVoting vProg from EMIT-VERIFIED (atelier template, predicate stub returning true) to COMPILE-VERIFIED (real RISC0 ballot-privacy guest) to SETTLED-LIVE on TN10. This COMPLETES ALL 5 of 5 vProg patterns settled live. An eligible voter casts ONE valid ballot adopted into a tally WITHOUT revealing the ballot — only the new tally root + a nullifier reach the journal. Same recipe as PoR (3d7c4a16...), ZK-KYC (52616496...), ConfidentialTransfer (2d8166f9...), BatchRollup (0237d2de...). Captured 2026-06-29; engine rusty-kaspa v2.0.0 (90dbf07). Local-only.
vprog_private_voting_settled_live.pattern: private-voting (ballot privacy: eligible voter casts one ballot into a tally without revealing it)
vprog_private_voting_settled_live.real_predicate: pv-guest (kii-csci-prover/methods/pv-guest/src/main.rs): over PRIVATE inputs read INSIDE the zkVM and NEVER journaled in the clear — voter_secret[32], ballot:u32 (0..K), prev_tally[K] — the guest asserts: (1) ELIGIBILITY: sha256(voter_secret) in ELECTORATE (fixed small set; v1, structured for a future Merkle membership root); (2) BALLOT VALIDITY: ballot < K; (3) TALLY UPDATE: claimed_next_tally == prev_tally with ONLY bucket `ballot` incremented by exactly 1 (all other buckets unchanged); (4) ONE-VOTE: nullifier = sha256(voter_secret || election_id), committed (so a repeat is detectable). Commits new_state_hash = sha256(tally_root || nullifier || epoch || new_seq), tally_root = sha256(next_tally bytes). The individual ballot is NEVER journaled — only the aggregate tally root + nullifier. Any failing clause PANICS the guest -> no proof.
vprog_private_voting_settled_live.sample_inputs: voter_secret=[0xA1;32] (eligible), ballot=2 (private), prev_tally=[10,20,30,40] -> claimed_next_tally=[10,20,31,40] (only bucket 2 +1), election_id=[0x55;32], epoch=0, prev_seq=0.
vprog_private_voting_settled_live.tally_advanced_by_1_positive_check: Observed in both the dev journal and the real proof: prev_tally [10,20,30,40] -> next_tally [10,20,31,40] — exactly bucket 2 (the private ballot) advanced by 1, every other bucket unchanged. The ballot value (2) is NOT in the journal; only the aggregate tally_root + nullifier are committed. Ballot privacy + correct tally update, simultaneously.

vprog_private_voting_settled_live.honest_scope: Proves eligibility + ballot-validity + tally update + commits a nullifier, with only the aggregate tally root + nullifier on-chain (the ballot stays private — genuinely cross-layer). RESIDUALS (v1, honest): (1) FIXED ELECTORATE set (sha256 of voter secrets), NOT a Merkle membership root — structured so it could later become one. (2) commits a nullifier but does NOT maintain a persistent nullifier SET across settlements — so cross-settlement double-vote prevention is a DOCUMENTED RESIDUAL (single-settlement double-vote detection only). (3) fixed sample inputs; prev_tally is a sample starting value, not anchored from a prior on-chain voting state. (4) the live covenant is the tag-0x21 verifier P2SH (image_id-pinned), enforcing 'a valid PV proof for this vProg' but NOT yet a silverscript state machine — same residual class as PoR/KYC/CT/BR (the C5-style next step).
vprog_private_voting_settled_live.honest_assessment: GENUINELY SETTLED LIVE. PrivateVoting moved emit-verified -> compile-verified -> settled-live: a real RISC0 ballot-privacy proof was verified in-consensus on TN10 (settle 112780ec..., is_accepted), with FOUR independent negative controls (guest-panic on ineligible-voter / out-of-range-ballot / wrong-tally-update; node ZK-reject on a tampered journal) PLUS the positive tally-advanced-by-1 check with the ballot kept private. This COMPLETES ALL 5 OF 5 vProg patterns settled live (proof-of-reserves, compliance-credential, confidential-transfer, batch-rollup, private-voting). The live broadcast required a long node IBD catch-up (the node had fallen ~75 min behind across the multi-hour proving sessions); I waited for is_synced=true before broadcasting and did not fake any txid.
vprog_private_voting_settled_live._note_completion: Earlier attempts at this task were interrupted by a transient server rate-limit and then a long node IBD; this run completed the live broadcast after the node reached is_synced=true. All txids are real, observed via submit_transaction + REST is_accepted (rejected negctl 404).

vprog_batch_rollup_settled_live._summary: Tier-3 milestone (4th vProg): graduated the BatchRollup vProg from EMIT-VERIFIED (atelier template, predicate stub returning true) to COMPILE-VERIFIED (real RISC0 N-transition fold guest) to SETTLED-LIVE on TN10. N->1 aggregation: a covenant cannot replay N transitions in one spend; the vProg proves the fold (applying N deltas to prev_root yields next_root) and the covenant verifies ONE proof, advancing seq by N in a single settlement. Same recipe as PoR (3d7c4a16...), ZK-KYC (52616496...), ConfidentialTransfer (2d8166f9...). Captured 2026-06-29; engine rusty-kaspa v2.0.0 (90dbf07). Local-only.
vprog_batch_rollup_settled_live.pattern: batch-rollup (N->1 aggregation: fold N state transitions into one proof)
vprog_batch_rollup_settled_live.real_predicate: br-guest (kii-csci-prover/methods/br-guest/src/main.rs): over PRIVATE inputs read INSIDE the zkVM and NEVER journaled individually — prev_root[32], deltas: Vec<u64> (the N transition deltas) — the guest folds: root=prev_root; for each delta: root=sha256(root||delta_le); next_root=root. It asserts: (1) batch_count >= 1 (non-empty); (2) batch_count == deltas.len() (N matches); (3) claimed_next_root == folded next_root (operator did not lie about the result); (4) claimed_new_seq == prev_seq + batch_count (seq advances by exactly N). Commits new_state_hash = sha256(next_root || epoch || new_seq) where new_seq = prev_seq + N. The chain sees ONLY next_root + the advanced seq — never the individual deltas. Any failing clause PANICS the guest -> no proof.
vprog_batch_rollup_settled_live.sample_inputs: prev_root=[0x22;32], deltas=[11,22,33,44,55] (N=5), epoch=0, prev_seq=0. Folded next_root=3c4d60b2b248b977f0c6d587abfe71c29a76754be87f73af0000020c44f7a4ca; new_seq=5.
vprog_batch_rollup_settled_live.seq_advanced_by_N_positive_check: The committed journal seq tail is 0500000000000000 (= 5) — i.e. ONE settlement advanced seq from 0 by exactly N=5 (the N->1 aggregation property), instead of the +1 a single transition would give. Observed in both the dev journal and the real proof journal.

RESIDUALS (v1, honest): (1) FIXED SAMPLE transitions and a sha256 CHAINED-FOLD root (root'=sha256(root||delta)), NOT a Merkle/Verkle accumulator with membership proofs. (2) prev_root is a sample starting value, not anchored from a prior on-chain rollup state. (3) the live covenant is the tag-0x21 verifier P2SH (image_id-pinned), enforcing 'a valid BR proof for this vProg' but NOT yet a silverscript state machine — same residual class as PoR/KYC/CT (the C5-style next step). vprog_confidential_transfer_settled_live._summary: Tier-3 milestone (3rd vProg): graduated the ConfidentialTransfer vProg from EMIT-VERIFIED (atelier template, predicate stub returning true) to COMPILE-VERIFIED (real RISC0 shielded-transfer guest) to SETTLED-LIVE on TN10. The moat piece: proves a transfer is valid over HIDDEN amounts (which a covenant fundamentally cannot do) + an optional regulator audit key. Same recipe as PoR (3d7c4a16...) and ZK-KYC (52616496...). Captured 2026-06-29; engine rusty-kaspa v2.0.0 (90dbf07). Local-only.
vprog_confidential_transfer_settled_live.pattern: confidential-transfer (shielded transfer over hidden amounts, with regulator audit key)
vprog_confidential_transfer_settled_live.real_predicate: ct-guest (kii-csci-prover/methods/ct-guest/src/main.rs): over PRIVATE inputs read INSIDE the zkVM and NEVER journaled in the clear — sender_old_balance, amount, recipient_old_balance, caller-asserted sender_new/recipient_new, blinding[32], audit_shared_secret[32] — the guest asserts: (1) RANGE: amount <= sender_old_balance (u64 => amount >= 0); (2a) sender_new == sender_old - amount; (2b) VALUE CONSERVATION over hidden amounts: (sender_old + recipient_old) == (sender_new + recipient_new) — rejects any value-creating transfer; (3) AUDIT BINDING: audit_ciphertext XOR keystream == amount. Commits new_state_hash = sha256(sender_commitment || recipient_commitment || audit_ciphertext(8) || epoch || new_seq), each commitment = sha256(balance || blinding). The chain sees ONLY commitments + the opaque audit ciphertext + epoch + seq — NEVER amounts or balances. Any failing clause PANICS the guest -> no proof.
vprog_confidential_transfer_settled_live.audit_mechanism: v1 symmetric stream-cipher pad: audit_ciphertext = amount_le(8) XOR keystream, keystream = sha256(audit_shared_secret || epoch)[..8]. The guest PROVES the same amount used in the conservation check is the one encrypted (audit binding). A regulator holding audit_shared_secret recovers the amount by XOR'ing the ciphertext with the same keystream. HONEST: this is a v1 symmetric-pad DEMONSTRATOR, NOT production ElGamal/Pedersen; the commitments are sha256(balance||blinding), not Pedersen commitments.
vprog_confidential_transfer_settled_live.sample_inputs: sender_old=1000000, amount=250000, recipient_old=500000 -> sender_new=750000, recipient_new=750000 (conserves: 1500000==1500000). audit_ciphertext=63df334876605302 (epoch 0).
vprog_confidential_transfer_settled_live.positive_audit_decrypt_check: decrypt(audit_ciphertext=63df334876605302, audit_shared_secret) = 250000 == amount (host-side, confirms a regulator recovers the exact transfer amount while the chain sees only the opaque ciphertext).

vprog_compliance_credential_settled_live._summary: Tier-3 milestone (2nd vProg): graduated the ComplianceCredential vProg from EMIT-VERIFIED (atelier template, predicate stub returning true) to COMPILE-VERIFIED (real RISC0 ZK-KYC selective-disclosure guest) to SETTLED-LIVE on TN10. Same recipe as proof-of-reserves (3d7c4a16...). Captured 2026-06-29; engine rusty-kaspa v2.0.0 (90dbf07). Local-only.
vprog_compliance_credential_settled_live.real_predicate: kyc-guest (kii-csci-prover/methods/kyc-guest/src/main.rs): over PRIVATE holder attributes read INSIDE the zkVM and NEVER committed — accredited:bool, jurisdiction_code:u32, identity_secret:[u8;32] — the guest asserts ALL of: (1) accredited == true; (2) jurisdiction_code in ALLOWED {840 US, 978 EU-MiCA, 702 SG}; (3) sha256(identity_secret) NOT in SANCTIONS (fixed array, structured for a future Merkle non-membership root). On success commits new_state_hash = sha256(credential_commitment || verdict=1 || epoch || new_seq) where credential_commitment = sha256(identity_secret || jurisdiction_code). The chain sees ONLY commitment+verdict+epoch+seq — NEVER the attributes (real selective disclosure). Any failing clause PANICS the guest -> no proof can exist.

A false predicate makes the guest PANIC → no proof can exist.

crux_combined_enforcement.question: Can ONE spend path enforce BOTH the silverscript covenant rules AND the tag-0x21 STARK verification?
crux_combined_enforcement.single_redeem_verdict: NOT EXPRESSIBLE on the pinned engine via a single silverscript-compiled redeem. silverc/portrait CANNOT emit OpZkPrecompile (portrait-emit/src/lib.rs literally carries `[PENDING: add OpZkPrecompile(0x21, journal) call when engine support lands]`). The compiled CsciInstrument.script uses OpInputCovenantId(0xcf)+OpCheckSig(0xac)+OpBlake2b(0xaa) but NOT OpZkPrecompile(0xa6). CsciInstrument.portrait states this explicitly as HONEST SCOPE: silverscript enforces the state-machine rules + cov-id binding; the proof-bytes verification is layered by the settlement harness.
crux_combined_enforcement.two_input_verdict: ACHIEVABLE and PROVEN against the real v2.0.0 engine (offline). One transaction with input[0] = CsciInstrument covenant UTXO (silverscript: seq/auth/cov-id) and input[1] = tag-0x21 P2SH(redeem) verifier UTXO (OpZkPrecompile over the real STARK). BOTH inputs were executed through TxScriptEngine with one shared CovenantsContext and BOTH ACCEPTED: input[0]=104898 script units, input[1]=25000176 script units. Since a tx is valid only if every input's script passes, this is genuine ATOMIC CO-ENFORCEMENT of both layers in one tx.
crux_combined_enforcement.combined_proof_command: KCP_MODE=combined KCP_CSCI_CAPTURE=<csci-capture.json> KCP_PROOF_DIR=<proof-export/succinct> KCP_KEY_FILE=.secrets/tn10-portrait.key cargo run -p portrait-settlement --bin csci_settle --release
crux_combined_enforcement.honest_cross_binding_gap: The two layers are CO-ENFORCED but NOT cryptographically CROSS-BOUND to the same identity. The silverscript require is `proof_cov_id == OpInputCovenantId(0)`, where OpInputCovenantId(0) returns the ENGINE per-instance covenant_id = covenant_id(funding_outpoint, [output]) (depends on the spending outpoint; differs every deployment). The STARK journal instead binds the sha256 KovId (88a73a9f...). These are DIFFERENT 32-byte values. So: the silverscript checks a spender-supplied proof_cov_id equals the runtime covenant id (real on-chain binding of the silverscript layer to its own UTXO), and the STARK independently binds the KovId — but nothing forces the STARK's covenant_id to equal the engine per-instance id. Fully closing this needs EITHER silverc emitting OpZkPrecompile (so one script both verifies the proof and checks its journal's covenant_id), OR committing the engine per-instance covenant_id into the journal (a build-time bootstrap, since it depends on the funding outpoint). Neither is available on the pinned engine/compiler today.
crux_combined_enforcement.combined_live: NOT broadcast live. The offline combined proof runs the IDENTICAL consensus engine code path (TxScriptEngine + CovenantsContext, covenants_enabled), so it is decisive engine-level evidence; a live combined broadcast (two genesis locks + a ~223 KB combined spend tx) was judged to add risk/cost without adding enforcement evidence beyond what the offline engine proof + the two already-live single-layer settlements establish. [UNVERIFIED on-chain as a single combined tx — proven at the engine level + both layers proven live separately.]

c5_silverscript_self_enforcing_covenant.honest_assessment_c5: PARTIALLY MET, materially advanced. The covenant now SELF-ENFORCES its state-machine rules ON-CHAIN live (seq-monotonicity + committed-owner auth proven by a live settle ACCEPT and a live seq-violation REJECT). The tag-0x21 STARK verification is also enforced at consensus (proven live in C-prior, and re-proven offline bound to the new KovId). Combined enforcement of BOTH in ONE atomic tx is PROVEN against the real v2.0.0 engine (2-input design) but NOT broadcast live, and crucially is co-enforced rather than cryptographically cross-bound (silverscript checks the engine per-instance covenant_id; the STARK binds the sha256 KovId — different values). REMAINING to a fully-combined, cross-bound DoD: (1) silverc support for OpZkPrecompile so one silverscript redeem both verifies the proof and binds its journal, OR a journal that commits the engine per-instance covenant_id; (2) a live combined 2-input broadcast.  [SUPERSEDED by `cross_bound` below — the journal now commits the engine per-instance covenant_id, closing the co-enforced-vs-cross-bound gap for the silverscript layer; option (1)'s second branch is now realized.]

cross_bound._summary: Closes the C5 co-enforced-vs-cross-bound gap IN-HOUSE (local-only; nothing sent to kaspanet; no compiler change). The STARK journal now commits the ENGINE PER-INSTANCE covenant_id (not the sha256 KovId), so the silverscript `require(proof_cov_id == OpInputCovenantId(0))` and the proof reference the SAME 32-byte id. Two-phase bootstrap (lock -> read per-instance covid -> prove bound to it -> spend). LIVE on testnet-10; engine rusty-kaspa v2.0.0 (90dbf07).
cross_bound.method: per-instance covenant_id = covenant_id(funding_outpoint, [genesis_output]) — what OpInputCovenantId(0) returns when the locked covenant UTXO is spent. The CSCI vProg guest takes covenant_id as an input (KCP_COVENANT_ID); we set it to the per-instance covid AFTER the lock, regenerate the real STARK (RISC0_DEV_MODE=0) so journal[0..32] == that per-instance covid, then spend.

phase3_cross_bound_settle.preflight_check: settle-bound mode reads the UTXO's ON-CHAIN covenant_id (e1e562...) and ASSERTS journal[0..32] == it before submitting; ACCEPT used_script_units=104898.
phase3_cross_bound_settle.what_it_proves: The silverscript require(proof_cov_id == OpInputCovenantId(0)) passes with proof_cov_id = e1e562... = OpInputCovenantId(0), AND the STARK journal commits the SAME e1e562.... Same 32-byte id on both sides → genuinely cross-bound (no longer two different values).
phase3_cross_bound_settle.verified_via: RpcApi submit_transaction (live) + api-tn10.kaspa.org (is_accepted=true) + spends the locked covenant UTXO.

cross_binding_negative_control.what: Took the valid proof bound to covid_A (e1e562...) and attempted to spend a DIFFERENT covenant instance B. OpInputCovenantId(0) returns covid_B != covid_A, so the silverscript require must reject — proving the binding is to the SPECIFIC covenant identity (distinct from the seq-violation reject and the ZK-integrity reject already shown).
cross_binding_negative_control.local_engine_reject: covenant engine rejected: VerifyError (the require(proof_cov_id == OpInputCovenantId(0)) fails: spliced covid_A != instance covid_B)
cross_binding_negative_control.node_error: RPC Server (remote error) -> Rejected transaction 60e9effc...: failed to verify the signature script: script ran, but verification failed

cross_bound.gap_now_closed: YES for the silverscript layer's covenant-id binding: the STARK journal commits the engine per-instance covenant_id, the exact value OpInputCovenantId(0) returns and the silverscript require checks. The cross-binding negative control proves a proof for one instance cannot satisfy another.
residual_honest: ARCHITECTURE: cross-binding holds WITHIN the silverscript covenant input. The tag-0x21 ZK *verification* of the STARK still runs as a SEPARATE input (the verifier P2SH(redeem), proven live in C-prior and offline-combined). The cross-bound live settle above (settle-bound) is a 1-input silverscript spend; it proves the journal commits the same per-instance covid the silverscript checks, but it does NOT itself run OpZkPrecompile. A single tx that BOTH runs the tag-0x21 verifier AND has the silverscript check the proof's journal covid against OpInputCovenantId(0) is the 2-input combined design (engine-proven offline in `combined` mode); a LIVE combined 2-input broadcast that also carries the per-instance-bound proof was not performed.

SEMANTIC: the silverscript checks proof_cov_id (a witness arg) == OpInputCovenantId(0). It does NOT itself verify a STARK exists for that journal — that is the tag-0x21 verifier's job. So a spender who knows the runtime covid could pass the silverscript require WITHOUT a valid proof; the proof's necessity comes from the verifier input. Full single-script binding (one script that both verifies the proof and binds its journal covid) still needs silverc OpZkPrecompile support, which is intentionally OFF THE TABLE (no upstream path). The in-house closure achieved here is: the proof's journal is now bound to the per-instance covid, so when the verifier input IS present (2-input combined), both layers reference the same identity.

combined_live_and_single_redeem._summary: Closes the two named residuals from `cross_bound`: (ITEM 1) the 2-input combined enforcement is now LIVE on TN10 in ONE transaction (no longer offline-only), cross-bound to the per-instance covid, with a live negative control; (ITEM 2) a single raw P2SH redeem doing BOTH layers was investigated honestly and REMAINS blocked on the v2.0.0 engine — reported with the exact limitation. Captured 2026-06-28; engine rusty-kaspa v2.0.0 (90dbf07). Local-only, nothing sent to kaspanet.

item1_combined_live.what: ONE transaction whose acceptance requires BOTH inputs to pass: input[0] = CsciInstrument silverscript covenant UTXO (seq/auth/cov-id binding), input[1] = tag-0x21 P2SH(redeem) verifier UTXO (OpZkPrecompile over the real STARK). Cross-bound: the covenant locked first, its per-instance covid read, the proof's journal commits that covid (verified before submit), and the verifier redeem is built from that proof.
item1_combined_live.inputs_confirmed: input[0] spends c6912cde...:0 (covenant), input[1] spends fab70e35...:0 (verifier) — both in the single mined tx (REST is_accepted=true).
item1_combined_live.offline_preflight: both inputs ACCEPT via the real v2.0.0 engine + CovenantsContext before submit: input[0]=104898 units, input[1]=25000176 units.
item1_combined_live.mass_engineering: compute mass = size*1 + spk*10 + 100*sum(compute_budget). The 222 KB seal costs ~223k size-mass; budgets sized tight (input[0]=14, input[1]=2510 budget units) to land mass=477206 < 500000. Fee 60M sompi (node requires ~47.7M for this mass).
item1_combined_live.verified_via: RpcApi submit_transaction (live) + api-tn10.kaspa.org (is_accepted=true, n_inputs=2, accepting_block present).

item1_negative_control.what: Same combined tx shape but the proof journal (input[1]) is tampered (journal[0]^=1) so the tag-0x21 STARK verification MUST fail; the covenant input[0] is otherwise valid. The whole tx must be rejected → proves BOTH layers are required.
item1_negative_control.node_error: RPC Server (remote error) -> Rejected transaction 1f49c3dc...: failed to verify the signature script: ZK Integrity: Verification failed

combined_live_and_single_redeem.item1_status: CLOSED — both layers enforced in ONE live transaction on TN10 is now a real on-chain fact (abc2d13f...), with a live negative control proving the conjunction.

item2_single_redeem.what: Investigated whether ONE raw P2SH redeem, composed in-house at the raw-script level, can run BOTH (a) the tag-0x21 OpZkPrecompile verify AND (b) the silverscript covenant bindings, in a single input.
item2_single_redeem.construction: composed redeem = [CsciInstrument compiled script (524 B)] OP_VERIFY [image_id][control_id][hashfn][tag] OpZkPrecompile (596 B total). Within engine limits (max_scripts_size=1_000_000, max_script_element_size=1_000_000). Tried BOTH orderings: variant A = covenant-first ([covenant] OP_VERIFY [zk]); variant B = zk-first ([zk] OP_VERIFY [covenant]).
item2_single_redeem.result: REJECTED in BOTH layouts on the real v2.0.0 engine (covenant engine rejected: VerifyError).
item2_single_redeem.exact_limitation: silverc emits the covenant as a SELF-CONTAINED P2SH redeem: it runs a selector dispatch (leader/delegate) and the leader success path executes TERMINAL OP_DROPs that clear the stack down to a single bool. So (variant A) it does not preserve the tag-0x21 satisfier (claim/control_index/control_digests/seal/journal) beneath its result for the appended zk verify to consume; and (variant B) it does not tolerate the extra items a prior zk verify leaves on the stack. It also never emits OpZkPrecompile (portrait-emit carries `[PENDING: add OpZkPrecompile(0x21, journal) ...]`). Concatenating the raw bytes therefore breaks the engine's stack/selector semantics. NOT a size or budget limit — it is the covenant script's stack discipline + the absent surface opcode.
item2_single_redeem.status: REMAINS — a clean single-script binding needs the silverscript-surface OpZkPrecompile opcode, which is intentionally OFF THE TABLE (no upstream/kaspanet). This is reported truthfully, not faked. The 2-input combined LIVE tx (item 1) is the in-house way to enforce both, and it stands.
item2_single_redeem.no_broadcast: No single-redeem tx was broadcast (the engine rejected it offline); honestly no txid.

files_changed_this_build.csci_settle_bin: examples/portrait-settlement/src/bin/csci_settle.rs — added modes combined-live, combined-negctl (item 1) and item2 (single composed redeem, both layouts).

what_makes_it_real: covenant_id is the REAL KovId of the covenant that locks the settled UTXO: blake2b256(redeem_script) = the exact 32-byte value the P2SH lock (OP_BLAKE2B <hash> OP_EQUAL) commits. Self-consistency verified: journal[0..32] == covid(image_id,control_id) == the P2SH commit.

new_state_hash is a genuine CsciState transition (50-byte encoding: KttState(42) || seq(8 LE)), seq 0 -> 1, owner = the funded TN10 wallet's real x-only pubkey, amount 500000 sompi. Pinned to kcp_csci::CsciState by crates/kcp-csci/tests/csci_smoke.rs::guest_state_and_rule_hash_match_library.
the guest computes the transition INSIDE the zkVM and commits the 104-byte journal; the host now feeds it the real covenant_id (env KCP_COVENANT_ID) and the real owner pubkey.
real_instrument.covenant_id_derivation: blake2b256(redeem), redeem = <image_id> <control_id> <hashfn=01> <tag=21> OpZkPrecompile(0xa6) = the engine's R0Fields::p2sh_scripts() redeem half. Derived by examples/portrait-settlement/src/bin/covid.rs via kcp_common::p2sh::redeem_script_hash.

real_proof.source: kii-csci-prover (RISC0_DEV_MODE=0, real STARK), two-pass: pass 1 discovers image_id+control_id, covid derives covenant_id, pass 2 binds it.

image_id_reproducibility.control_id_note: control_id (c9b08054...) is the RISC Zero recursion-program identity; it is stable across guest changes (observed identical before and after the guest's 50-byte-state fix) and across runs.
image_id_reproducibility.determinism_evidence: Rebuilt the guest clean (touch + cargo build) across three independent runs (dev, real pass-1, real pass-2) — all produced image_id c6ce0eda....

toolchain.risc0_zkvm_and_build: 3.0.5 (pinned in kii-csci-prover Cargo.lock and methods/csci-guest Cargo.lock)

image_id_reproducibility.rebuild_command: cd kii-csci-prover && touch methods/csci-guest/src/main.rs && RISC0_DEV_MODE=1 cargo run --release -p csci-prover | grep 'image id'
image_id_reproducibility.covenant_commits_image_id: The redeem (71 bytes) embeds image_id; the P2SH lock commits blake2b256(redeem). Therefore the covenant accepts a spend only for a tag-0x21 proof whose image_id == c6ce0eda... — confirmed offline by portrait-settlement [3/3] (wrong image id REJECTED) and on-chain by the settle acceptance.

settle.note: Accepted by consensus ONLY because OpZkPrecompile verified the real STARK and bound covenant_id=869bfb4d... journal=2c340c7e... image_id=c6ce0eda... control_id=c9b08054.... covenant_id == blake2b256(this covenant's redeem), so the proof settles the very covenant that locked the UTXO.
settle.verified_via: RpcApi submit_transaction (live) + api-tn10.kaspa.org (is_accepted=true) + P2SH UTXO poll (settle UTXO spent; only the negctl UTXO remains).

negative_control.type: ON-CHAIN — submitted to the live node with the local preflight disabled (spend_p2sh_tx_with_sigops skip_preflight=true).
negative_control.node_error: RPC Server (remote error) -> Rejected transaction e44d4f7bf5ac33d498dda6e6750460336beb380618c6c35fdd7221e0ca4d7d32: failed to verify the signature script: ZK Integrity: Verification failed
negative_control.negctl_utxo_status: UNSPENT — the P2SH address still shows 1 UTXO (the negctl lock); the tampered spend never consumed it.
negative_control._why_null: A rejected transaction has no on-chain txid. The evidence of consensus enforcement is the live node's reject error + the 404 + the unspent UTXO.

fixes_made.guest_real_state: kii-csci-prover/methods/csci-guest/src/main.rs: computes the genuine 50-byte CsciState (KttState 42 + seq 8 LE) instead of a 42-byte state; new_state_hash now matches kcp_csci::CsciState::state_hash().
fixes_made.guest_real_covenant_id: guest reads covenant_id as input (already did); host (kii-csci-prover/host/src/main.rs) now passes a real covenant_id via KCP_COVENANT_ID and the real owner pubkey, exports covenant_id.hex, and prints all inputs.
fixes_made.covid_bin: examples/portrait-settlement/src/bin/covid.rs derives covenant_id = blake2b256(redeem) from a proof dir.
fixes_made.negctl_onchain: crates/kcp-common/src/p2sh.rs: spend_p2sh_tx_with_sigops gained skip_preflight; settle.rs now locks a 2nd P2SH UTXO and submits the tampered spend to the live node to capture the node's rejection.
fixes_made.guest_library_agreement_test: crates/kcp-csci/tests/csci_smoke.rs::guest_state_and_rule_hash_match_library pins guest new_state_hash/rule_hash to the library types.
fixes_made.pubkey_example: crates/kcp-common/examples/pubkey.rs prints a wallet's x-only pubkey.
fixes_made.prior_run_pieces: kcp-common p2sh covenant flags + explicit sigop budget + journal-hash fallback (from the first Phase A run) are retained.

honest_caveats: The covenant locking script is a generic P2SH wrapping the tag-0x21 verifier (image_id+control_id pinned). The CSCI-specific .sil locking script (seq monotonicity / state-continuity enforced ON-CHAIN) is not yet authored; seq/continuity are currently enforced inside the vProg, not by silverscript. The proof IS bound to the covenant program identity, but the covenant does not yet itself re-derive/enforce the CSCI state machine on-chain.

covenant_id = blake2b256(redeem) is the P2SH program identity (KIP-20-style), not a silverscript-bytecode KovId, because the CSCI .sil does not exist yet. This is documented and self-consistent with what the chain commits.
Genesis prev state (amount 1_000_000, seq 0) is a chosen starting point, not itself anchored on-chain by a prior settle (this is the first transition of the instance).
negative_control_node_error: Rejected transaction e44d4f7bf5ac33d498dda6e6750460336beb380618c6c35fdd7221e0ca4d7d32: failed to verify the signature script: ZK Integrity: Verification failed
